MINI MINUTES: Appearance for Plaintiff and Defendant. Case has been mediated. The OSC is ordered off calendar. Proceedings reported by: Brenda Fields. (212)

Mtc Of Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery & For Order Establishing Admissions & For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-20-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
Plaintiff's motion for evidentiary sanctions or to compel complete responses to requests for admissions and special interrogatories -- Granted in part and denied in part.
The motion for evidentiary sanctions is denied.

The motion for complete and verified responses to requests for admissions 2, 4, 6, 11, 17 is denied as moot in light of the supplemental responses. The Court makes no rulings as to the sufficiency of the responses.

The motion for complete and verified responses to special interrogatories 3 - 5, 7, 11, 13 is denied as moot in light of the supplemental responses. The reservation of the right to amend the responses in the preface is stricken. A response must be complete at the time it is made. If there is a need to amend, it must be made in compliance with CCP Section 2030(m).

The Court notes the delay by defendant in serving complete verified responses. However, the request for monetary sanctions is denied as the Court finds the imposition of sanctions to be unjust under the circumstances set forth in the moving papers. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-20-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
Defendant's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories -- Granted in part and denied in part.
#1, 2, 4: The objections are overruled and stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to provide further responses.
#3: The question is rewritten to clarify that the person most knowledgeable refers to someone other than plaintiff. The objections are overruled and stricken. Plaintiff is ordered to provide a further response.
#5: Denied. The objections are overruled and stricken. The answer is otherwise sufficient.

Plaintiff asserted a number of unmeritorious objections and made evasive responses. However, the request for monetary sanctions is denied as the Court finds the imposition of sanctions to be unjust under the circumstances set forth in the moving papers. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions

MINI MINUTES:
Defendant David Bellings' motion to compel plaintiff's further responses to discovery and for sanctions -- continued to May 20, 3005 at 9:00 a.m. to be heard with plaintiff's motion. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
